commands.notes: Target workpack passes protocol lint checks.
All required workpack files and prompt outputs are present.

verification.regression_notes: Retrospective/state/status updates only; no product runtime changes.

handoff.summary: Retrospective completed with scope delivery summary, rework/friction assessment, estimation analysis, and 5 owned action items. Zero B-series fixes required. Major process finding: codex agents failed to commit per-prompt, forcing V1 gate remediation.
next_steps: Open PR from feature/npm-distribution to main.
Track AI-1 through AI-5 in the next protocol/tooling planning cycle.
known_issues: A0_bootstrap status remained 'pending' in workpack.state.json despite completion — state tracking gap.
A1-A4 output JSONs all declare commit_shas=[570c08d] but that commit only contains A0 bootstrap output; actual deliverables were committed as 2749325 by V1 gate.
Execution telemetry fields (tokens_in, tokens_out, duration_ms) are zeroed in all output artifacts.

notes:
1. Scope and delivery summary
- Delivered full request scope: monorepo structure (pnpm-workspace.yaml, packages/protocol, packages/extension), @workpack/protocol npm package with schemas/templates/agent-docs/tools, init CLI with --dir and --protocol-version flags, VS Code extension scaffold with TypeScript activation entry point.
- All 14 acceptance criteria (AC1-AC14) verified as met by V1 integration gate.
- 3 feature branch commits: 570c08d (A0 bootstrap), 2749325 (A1-A4 bulk delivery), b146322 (V1 gate).

Metrics
| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| Total prompts | 7 planned / 7 completed (A0-A4, V1, R1) |
| First-pass completions | 6 (A0-A4 + V1; R1 in progress) |
| B-series count | 0 |
| Total elapsed time | 2d 12h wall-clock (2026-02-23T22:29 creation -> 2026-02-26T10:30 V1 complete); ~1h 43m active agent execution |

2. What went well
- All 4 A-series prompts completed on first pass with zero B-series corrective prompts required.
- A2_protocol_package and A4_extension_scaffold were correctly parallelized per plan (both depend only on A1), saving elapsed time.
- 189/189 existing tests passed throughout all prompts — zero regressions introduced.
- A3_init_command added its own integration test (test-init.js), correctly growing regression coverage.
- npm pack dry-run verified protocol package bundling with 35 files.
- TypeScript extension scaffold compiled cleanly on first attempt.
- V1 gate systematically verified all 14 ACs with documented evidence.

3. What caused friction
- Codex agents did not commit their work: A1-A4 left all code uncommitted in the working tree. V1 gate discovered this and had to create remediation commit 2749325 containing all 53 files from A1-A4.
- A0_bootstrap.status remained 'pending' in workpack.state.json despite the prompt being executed and output JSON existing. State tracking was not updated by the bootstrap agent.
- A1-A4 output JSONs all incorrectly reference commit SHA 570c08d (the A0 bootstrap commit) instead of their actual work commit. This is a metadata integrity failure.
- 8 pre-existing linter errors in other workpacks created noise in baseline verification (not in scope but confusing).
- Planned base branch was 'main' but repository default is 'master' — noted in A0 known issues but not resolved.

4. Defects and rework analysis
- Code defects requiring B-series: none (0 B-series prompts).
- Metadata defect: commit SHA tracking fundamentally broken across all codex-executed prompts. All A1-A4 outputs reference wrong SHA.
- Process defect: V1 gate absorbed remediation work (committing all outstanding code) that should have been done per-prompt.
- Impact: branch_verified=false in V1 output because commit SHAs in A1-A4 outputs don't match actual change commits.

5. Estimation accuracy (planned vs actual)
| Prompt | Planned | Actual | Assessment |
|--------|---------|--------|------------|
| A0_bootstrap | XS | ~instant | Accurate |
| A1_monorepo_structure | M | 7m 30s | Over-estimated |
| A2_protocol_package | M | 3m 17s | Over-estimated |
| A3_init_command | M | 8m 32s | Over-estimated |
| A4_extension_scaffold | S | 3m 34s | Accurate |
| V1_integration_meta | S | 30m | Under-estimated (remediation work) |
Overall: A-series prompts ran significantly faster than M estimates. V1 gate was heavier than S estimate due to absorbing commit remediation.

6. Action items with owners
- AI-1: Enforce per-prompt commit discipline — each A-series prompt must commit its deliverables before producing output JSON. Owner: agent rules / AGENT_RULES.md maintainers.
- AI-2: Add commit SHA verification to output JSON validation — reject outputs where commit_shas don't reference commits containing the declared changed files. Owner: tooling maintainers (verify_commits.py).
- AI-3: Ensure A0_bootstrap always updates workpack.state.json to status='complete'. Owner: bootstrap prompt template maintainers.
- AI-4: Resolve main vs master branch discrepancy at repository level (rename default branch or update protocol templates). Owner: repo admin.
- AI-5: Recalibrate M-series effort estimates downward for codex-driven file creation prompts. Owner: workpack planners.

Commit SHA handling
- artifacts.commit_shas references b146322 (latest verified branch HEAD at V1 gate completion). R1 file changes are currently uncommitted in the working tree.
